description: Multi-agent AI pipeline backend for a pet companion Flutter iOS app
how_to_use: Mark 'complete' true only after feature is written AND manually tested. Mark 'tested' true only after the test suite passes for that feature. Never skip ahead — finish and test each feature before starting the next.
current_focus: Phase 3 (nightly batch jobs) + HistoryBuilder (ft-013) + Valkey hot storage (ft-005). All Sprint 2-3 and Sprint 4 review debt resolved. 68/70 E2E tests pass (2 LLM non-determinism).
storage_strategy: PostgreSQL (Phase 1C complete). Phase 2 adds write-through message persistence: app.state.sessions[thread_id] (in-memory) + thread_messages table (PostgreSQL). Active threads reloaded from DB on startup (W12: only loads un-compacted messages). Thread compaction via LLM summarizer at 50 messages. Per-thread asyncio.Lock for session mutations (C2). Per-pet asyncio.Lock for thread creation (Sprint 5). FK constraint: thread_messages → threads with CASCADE (Sprint 5). Partial unique index: one active thread per pet (Sprint 5). Graceful shutdown via tracked background tasks (W8). AALDA API fallback chain: fresh cache → API → expired cache → DB → error (W1+W10). Valkey hot storage deferred (ft-005).

phases.name: MVP — Agent 1 End-to-End
phases.description: Get a user talking to Agent 1 and receiving a real response. No database, no Redis, no Agent 2/3. Pet context (Luna + Shara) is hardcoded. This phase is done when you can send a message via Postman or React UI and get an intelligent response back.
phases.goal: POST /chat works. Agent 1 generates a real Azure OpenAI response. Guardrails filter it. Response returned.

features.name: requirements.txt — minimal dependencies
features.notes: pip install -r requirements.txt completes without errors. Verified.

features.name: .env.example — environment variable template
features.notes: Committed with placeholder values only. .env is gitignored.

features.name: constants.py — all business logic constants
features.notes: All regex patterns pre-compiled at module load. No secrets. Safe to commit.

features.name: app/core/config.py — environment config
features.notes: pydantic-settings BaseSettings. get_settings() with lru_cache — created once at startup.

features.name: app/llm/base.py — abstract LLM provider
features.notes: Strategy pattern — agent never knows which LLM it is talking to.

features.name: app/llm/azure_openai.py — Azure OpenAI implementation
features.notes: Verified: live Azure call returned a real response. health_check() returns True.

features.name: app/llm/factory.py — provider factory
features.notes: Only place that decides which LLM implementation to use.

features.name: dummy_context.py — hardcoded Luna + Shara test data
features.notes: DELETED — replaced by context_builder.py + JSON files in data/. Same defaults auto-seeded on first run.

features.name: app/services/guardrails.py — response filtering
features.notes: Phase 1A: IntentFlags and classify_intent() removed. Now only apply_guardrails() remains. One job: clean Agent 1 output.

features.name: app/agents/conversation.py — Agent 1
features.notes: Phase 1A: accepts intent_type str instead of IntentFlags. _build_flag_section() injects HEALTH/FOOD INTENT DETECTED block into system prompt.

features.name: app/main.py — FastAPI app + POST /chat
features.notes: Phase 1A: IntentClassifier wired in. ChatResponse simplified. Deeplink payload added.

features.name: Manual test: full Phase 0 verification
features.notes: Tested via React UI + curl. Frontend simplified: removed all screens except Chat.

phases.name: IntentClassifier + Deeplink Routing
phases.description: Replace unreliable regex intent detection with an LLM-based IntentClassifier that runs before Agent 1 on every request. Wire up health and food redirect logic so the mobile app can route users to specialist modules.
phases.goal: Health and food messages get short empathetic responses + redirect payload. General messages get full Agent 1 response. Classifier handles negation, past tense, and positive vet visits correctly.

features.name: app/agents/intent_classifier.py — LLM-based classifier
features.description: IntentClassifier class. Calls LLM with temperature=0.0, max_tokens=48. Returns (intent_type, urgency) tuple. Retry on bad JSON or low confidence. Immediate fallback on API error.
features.notes: Tested with 7 edge cases including negation and past-tense. All correct. Verified via debug_flow.py.

features.name: Remove IntentFlags and classify_intent() — Option B cleanup
features.description: Delete IntentFlags dataclass, classify_intent() function, and all entity regex logic from guardrails.py. guardrails.py now has one job: clean Agent 1 output.
features.notes: Removed HEALTH_URGENCY_HIGH_KEYWORDS, HEALTH_URGENCY_MEDIUM_KEYWORDS, FOOD_INTENT_KEYWORDS from constants.py. All dead code gone.

features.name: Health and food redirect / deeplink logic
features.description: build_deeplink() in deeplink.py returns DeeplinkPayload for health/food intents. ChatResponse includes redirect: RedirectPayload | None. Simulator pages at /health/chat and /food/chat.
features.notes: Tested health redirect via React UI. Redirect payload appears in response. Urgency=high shown in red on simulator page.

features.name: Wire IntentClassifier into chat route
features.description: main.py calls IntentClassifier.classify() before Agent 1. Passes intent_type to agent.run(). Passes intent_type + urgency to build_deeplink().
features.notes: Log line: Chat complete — session=... | intent=health | urgency=high | ...

features.name: Fix Agent 1 prompt direction bug
features.description: RULES_TEXT Rules 7 and 8 referenced 'flags below' but flag_section renders above RULES in the template. LLM looked below, found nothing, ignored the instruction. Fixed to 'flags above'.
features.notes: Root cause of 'agent ignores health intent' bug. After fix: health messages get 1-2 sentence empathy-only response as intended.

phases.name: Agent 2 (Compressor) + Agent 3 (Aggregator) — File-Based Storage
phases.description: Build fact extraction and aggregation pipeline using JSON files on disk. No PostgreSQL yet — logic first, infrastructure later. Storage interface designed so swapping in Postgres later requires zero changes to agent code.
phases.goal: After a conversation, extracted facts are saved to fact_log.json and merged into active_profile.json. Next session, Agent 1 can be given richer context from accumulated facts.

features.name: app/agents/state.py — AgentState dataclass
features.description: Shared context carrier for the background agent pipeline. Populated incrementally: main.py sets session_id + pet fields, ConversationAgent sets is_entity, Compressor sets extracted_facts + low_confidence_fields.
features.notes: Plain dataclass — no framework. low_confidence_fields is replaced each turn, never accumulated across requests.

features.name: app/storage/file_store.py — JSON file storage helpers
features.description: append_fact_log() and read_fact_log(). Atomic write pattern: write to .tmp then os.replace() — original file untouched if process dies mid-write.
features.notes: append_fact_log() tested via e2e suite. Atomic write verified. data/ directory auto-created.

features.name: app/agents/compressor.py — Agent 2
features.description: CompressorAgent class. is_entity gate: exits immediately if Agent 1 says no facts. Calls LLM (temp=0.0, max_tokens=400). Returns list of ExtractedFact with confidence >= 0.50. Caller splits by 0.70 threshold.
features.notes: 18/18 e2e tests pass. Handles: single/multiple facts, negative facts, past-tense time_scope, vet-confirmed source_rank, hedged confidence, full schema validation. Minor output schema updates pending (PRD review).

features.name: Wire Agent 1 is_entity + Compressor fire-and-forget in POST /chat
features.description: Agent 1 outputs {reply, is_entity} JSON. AgentState built per request. asyncio.create_task(_run_background) after reply sent. Compressor wired. Aggregator call pending (Aggregator not built yet).
features.notes: Agent 1 JSON output + _parse_agent_response() added. _run_background() splits facts by 0.70 threshold, writes to fact_log.json.

features.name: Debug fields in ChatResponse + GET /debug/facts endpoint
features.description: POST /chat response now includes is_entity, intent_type, urgency. GET /debug/facts?session_id=xxx returns Compressor output from fact_log.json filtered by session.
features.notes: debug/facts endpoint also used by frontend console.log for Agent 2 visibility.

features.name: tests/run_e2e.py — automated end-to-end test suite
features.description: 18 tests across 4 sections: infrastructure, intent routing, session management, Compressor fact extraction. Each test uses a unique session_id — facts in fact_log.json are traceable by session.
features.notes: All 18 tests pass. Covers: non-fact skip, single/multiple facts, negative fact, past-tense, vet-confirmed, hedged confidence, needs_clarification flag, full schema.

features.name: app/agents/aggregator.py — Agent 3
features.description: Aggregator class. Pure deterministic logic — no LLM. Conflict resolution Rules 0-6. Reads active_profile.json, merges high-confidence facts, writes back atomically.
features.notes: Rules 0-6 implemented. user_correction source_rank added to Compressor prompt. Wired into _run_background(). GET /debug/profile endpoint added. 6 Aggregator-specific e2e tests pass (24 total).

features.name: Compressor output schema — PRD review
features.description: Review output field types and taxonomy against the PRD before Aggregator is built on top of them.
features.notes: PRD reviewed — on track. No blocking changes needed before Aggregator build.

features.name: Data model — dataclasses for PetProfile, ActiveProfileEntry, UserProfile
features.description: Three dataclasses in app/models/context.py mirroring future PostgreSQL tables. Each has to_dict() and from_dict(). ActiveProfileEntry handles both simple seed format and full Aggregator format.
features.notes: 11 fields on ActiveProfileEntry including PRD conflict resolution fields (status, change_detected, trend_flag). Confidence auto-normalized from 0-100 to 0.0-1.0.

features.name: context_builder.py — replaces dummy_context.py
features.description: Reads pet_profile.json, active_profile.json, user_profile.json from data/. Returns same 5 values Agent 1 needs. Seeds Luna+Shara defaults on first run. Computes pet_summary and gap_list at runtime.
features.notes: dummy_context.py deleted. 18/18 e2e tests pass. JSON files auto-seeded on first request.

features.name: file_store.py — read/write for pet, active, user profiles
features.description: Added 6 functions (read/write for each profile type) plus internal _read_json/_write_json helpers. Same atomic write pattern as fact_log.
features.notes: read functions return None (not {}) when file missing. Aggregator must handle None.

features.name: constants.py — FULL_FIELD_LIST + expanded FIELD_LABELS
features.description: Added 8 new field labels (sex, food_brand, appetite, etc.). Added FULL_FIELD_LIST for gap computation. Added GAP_QUESTION_HINTS for new askable fields.
features.notes: Compressor prompt key names aligned with FULL_FIELD_LIST (weight not weight_kg, medications not current_medications).

features.name: Aggregator design doc
features.description: Full design document with conflict resolution rules, sub-phase plan, data model section, and interface specification.
features.notes: 13 sections. Rules 0-6 in priority order. Three sub-phases: A (core merge), B (gap list), C (confidence bar).

features.name: Route refactor — split main.py into app/routes/
features.description: Extracted POST /chat, debug endpoints, and simulator endpoints from main.py into separate route modules using FastAPI APIRouter. Shared state via app.state instead of module globals.
features.notes: main.py reduced to ~120 lines (app creation, CORS, lifespan, /health). All agents/sessions on app.state, accessed via request.app.state in routes. 21/24 e2e tests pass (2 pre-existing LLM flakes, 1 transient).

features.name: Confidence calculator — confidence bar in ChatResponse
features.description: calculate_confidence_score() computes 0-100 score from three signals: per-field confidence (Compressor/Aggregator), time decay (exponential, per-field category + life stage), and importance weight (Tier A=3, B=2, C=1). 22 scored fields, 4 decay categories (static/slow/medium/fast), life stage multipliers for puppy through senior. confidence_color() maps to green (80-100) / yellow (50-79) / red (0-49).
features.notes: Pure arithmetic, sub-ms, no LLM. Computed on-the-fly each request (not persisted — decay is time-dependent). Dropped PRD 'depth' component — per-field confidence already captures quality. Design doc at design-docs/confidence-bar.md.

features.name: Frontend confidence bar — wire ConfidenceBar into Chat UI
features.description: Connected existing ConfidenceBar component (compact variant) to /chat response. Shows confidence_score + confidence_color in the chat header next to pet name.
features.notes: ConfidenceBar.jsx and ConfidenceBar.css already existed. Only Chat.jsx changed: import, 2 state vars, update from API response, render in header. ~6 lines added.

features.name: Prompt v2 — PRD-aligned system prompt rewrite
features.description: Rewrote Agent 1 system prompt to align with PW1-PRD v0.2b. Identity, tone, emoji budget (max 2), pet suffix rules (-chan/-kun), priority ladder gap questions (Rank A-D bilingual), soft redirect for health/food, emergency override, prompt injection defense. MAX_QUESTIONS_PER_SESSION reduced 5→3. GAP_QUESTION_HINTS replaced with GAP_PRIORITY_LADDER.
features.notes: 22/24 e2e tests pass. 2 failures are pre-existing Compressor LLM flakes, unrelated to prompt changes. language_str hardcoded to EN — auto-detect deferred to Phase 2.

features.name: Reviewer feedback v1 — 6 issues fixed
features.description: Fixed Issues 2,3,4,5,7 from product team reviewer-feedback-v1-proposal.md. Issue 2: response structure rewritten (end with exactly 1 follow-up question). Issue 3: HARD RULE #5 — pet name as explicit subject. Issue 4: asked_gap_question field added to Agent 1 JSON output, replacing ? counting. Issue 5: language detection via Unicode range check (Hiragana/Katakana/CJK). Issue 7: emoji rule tightened to first mention only. Food urgency gating: LOW urgency food intents no longer redirect.
features.notes: asked_gap_question: bool added to AgentResponse. _detect_language() in chat.py uses Unicode ranges. Food intent with urgency=low returns no redirect. Medium urgency food gated by cooldown.

features.name: In-memory profile optimization — app.state read, write-through to disk
features.description: Eliminated per-request disk I/O. Profiles loaded from JSON once at startup into app.state. All runtime reads come from memory. Aggregator mutates shared dict by reference and writes through to disk for persistence. context_builder.py parameterized with optional in-memory profiles. Backward compatible (None falls back to disk read).
features.notes: load_profiles() called once in lifespan(). build_context() accepts optional active_profile_raw, pet_profile, user_profile params. Dict passed by reference — Aggregator mutations visible to all readers immediately. asyncio.Lock prevents concurrent mutations.

features.name: GET /confidence endpoint — dedicated confidence score API
features.description: Standalone GET /confidence endpoint so frontend can fetch score on mount without waiting for a /chat response. Reads from app.state (in-memory). Returns {confidence_score, confidence_color}.
features.notes: Frontend calls fetchConfidence() on mount and 4s after each message. Sticky redirect nudge replaces per-message inline buttons.

features.name: Code review + quick fixes
features.description: Code review of all backend files. Fixed 5 issues: stale comments (2), fragile mutation in context_builder.py, stale import reference in deeplink.py docstring, unused import cleanup.
features.notes: Deferred: dead code in constants.py (ENTITY_PATTERNS, MEDICAL_KEYWORDS, NUTRITIONAL_KEYWORDS) — cleanup tracked separately.

features.name: Frontend Agent 3 console.log
features.description: Added Agent 3 (Aggregator) debug logging in browser console. Fetches /debug/profile 8s after each message, logs field values with confidence and status.
features.notes: Amber color (#d97706) in console. Shows field_count, per-field value/confidence/status.

phases.name: Real Database — Swap JSON Files for PostgreSQL
phases.description: Replace file-based JSON storage with real PostgreSQL. context_builder.py already exists (reads JSON now, will read Postgres later). Agent 2 and Agent 3 code does not change.
phases.goal: Profile facts extracted from conversation, stored in Postgres, loaded for next session.

features.name: docker-compose.yml — PostgreSQL container
features.notes: PostgreSQL 16 Alpine on port 5433 (not 5432 — avoids conflict with local installs). Named volume pgdata for persistence.

features.name: Add DB packages to requirements.txt
features.notes: Added: sqlalchemy[asyncio], asyncpg, alembic.

features.name: Add DATABASE_URL to config + env files
features.notes: DATABASE_URL in Settings with default pointing to local Docker container. .env.example uses placeholder credentials.

features.name: app/db/ — session, ORM models, repositories
features.notes: 4 tables: pets, users, active_profile, fact_log. Repository pattern — same dict interface as file_store.py. Agents unchanged.

features.name: Alembic setup + migrations
features.notes: Two migrations: initial tables + indexes/field_key widening (from review). alembic upgrade head creates all tables.

features.name: context_builder.py — load_profiles_from_db()
features.notes: New load_profiles_from_db() reads from PostgreSQL, seeds Luna+Shara defaults on first run. Old load_profiles() kept for backward compat but deprecated.

features.name: main.py — DB init in lifespan + shutdown grace period
features.notes: init_db() at startup. asyncio.sleep(2) before dispose_engine() — lets background tasks finish writes. Aggregator receives get_session factory.

features.name: aggregator.py — write-through to PostgreSQL
features.notes: Constructor receives get_session factory. After merging facts, writes active_profile to DB. Rules 0-6 unchanged.

features.name: chat.py — FactLog writes to PostgreSQL
features.notes: _run_background() creates own session, appends facts to fact_log table via FactLogRepo.

features.name: debug.py — reads from PostgreSQL
features.notes: GET /debug/facts and GET /debug/profile now query PostgreSQL via repositories.

features.name: Code review + fixes (2 reviews combined)
features.notes: Fixed: alembic.ini credentials, missing fact_log indexes, bulk insert, seed data float types, dead imports, stale docstrings, shutdown grace period, DEFAULT_PET_ID constant, __repr__ on models.

features.name: e2e tests — updated for PostgreSQL (30 tests)
features.notes: facts_for() now calls GET /debug/facts instead of reading fact_log.json. 6 new PostgreSQL-specific tests. 29/30 pass (1 LLM flake).

phases.name: Thread & Conversation Management
phases.description: Implement conversation threads with 24-hour hard boundary. Persist every message to PostgreSQL (write-through). Replace the raw session dict in app.state.sessions with a proper Thread model. Redis/KeyVal deferred — decision pending (see ft-005).
phases.goal: Every message persisted to PostgreSQL. 24h thread boundary enforced. Server restart does not lose conversation history. Agent 1 still reads from in-memory hot storage (same pattern as active_profile).
phases.review: design-docs/phase-2-review-report.md — 4 critical, 8 warnings, 5 suggestions. See ft-006.
decisions: Thread = 24h hard deadline from started_at (not idle timeout). Thread closed at started_at + 24h regardless of activity.
Write-through pattern: append to app.state.sessions[thread_id] (memory) AND insert to thread_messages table (PostgreSQL) on every message.
Read from memory (app.state.sessions) — no DB hit on hot path.
On server restart: reload active thread messages from PostgreSQL back into memory (same pattern as active_profile reload).
Redis deferred — in-memory dict stays as hot storage for now. Redis replaces it later (ft-005).

features.name: threads + thread_messages ORM models + migration
features.notes: Thread model: thread_id (UUID unique), pet_id, user_id, started_at, expires_at, status, compaction_summary. ThreadMessage model: thread_id, role, content, timestamp. Both indexed. Hand-written migration (Docker wasn't running for autogenerate).

features.name: ThreadRepo + ThreadMessageRepo
features.notes: ThreadRepo: create(), get_active(), get_all_active(), expire(), update_compaction_summary(), get_latest_expired(). ThreadMessageRepo: append(), append_batch(), read_thread(). Same constructor injection pattern.

features.name: Thread boundary logic + write-through + compaction in chat.py
features.notes: Thread boundary: create/reuse/expire threads per pet. Write-through: in-memory sync + DB async via _run_background(). Compaction: _run_compaction() at 50 messages. ChatResponse gains thread_id + new_thread. AgentState gains thread_id.

features.name: Reload active threads on startup
features.notes: Startup: load active threads + messages from PostgreSQL into app.state.sessions. ThreadSummarizer initialized on app.state.

features.name: Thread summarizer service
features.notes: LLM summarizer (temp=0.0, max_tokens=400). Handles in-session compaction + cross-thread continuity. Fire-and-forget.

features.name: Wire conversation_summary into Agent 1
features.notes: build_context() returns 6 values (added conversation_summary pass-through). Agent 1 prompt injects CONVERSATION SUMMARY section before pet context.

features.name: Debug endpoints + CLAUDE.md + tests
features.notes: GET /debug/threads + GET /debug/thread/{id}/messages. CLAUDE.md fully updated. 10 new e2e tests (Section 7). 38/40 pass (2 pre-existing LLM flakes).

phases.name: Background Jobs
phases.description: Session compaction and nightly passive context gathering.

features.name: Session compaction job
features.notes: After N sessions: LLM summarizes conversation_log into compressed_history.

features.name: Nightly passive context batch
features.notes: Scan Health + Food module logs. Run Agent 2+3 pipeline on them.

phases.name: Auth + Security
phases.description: JWT auth for Flutter app. Rate limiting. Lock down CORS.

features.name: Rate limiting on POST /chat
features.notes: slowapi. 20 messages per IP per minute.

phases.name: Testing + Deployment
phases.description: Unit tests, integration tests, Docker, production deploy.

features.name: Unit tests — aggregator conflict resolution rules
features.notes: Most important unit test — pure logic, fully deterministic, no LLM needed.

features.name: Integration tests — POST /chat pipeline
features.notes: Full pipeline test with MockLLMProvider injected.

deployment_dev.name: Development Deployment (Railway/Render) — TEMPORARY
deployment_dev.description: Dev deployment setup for testing. DevOps team will replace with production pipeline later.

features.name: nixpacks.toml — Railway Python detection
features.notes: TEMPORARY. Added so Railway detects Python project. DevOps will replace.

features.notes: TEMPORARY. COPY paths use backend/ prefix (repo-root build context). Port hardcoded to 8000. DevOps will replace with production Dockerfile.

features.name: OPENAI_API_KEY — manual key verification
features.notes: TEMPORARY. Key added to .env for manual testing (2026-03-13, confirmed working). App uses Azure OpenAI, not direct OpenAI. Key not used by app code. Remove or wire into factory.py later.

features.name: demo/real-pet-context branch — merged to master
features.notes: Branch added real pet data from AALDA API + Dockerfile fixes. Merged to master for deployment testing. 6 commits: nixpacks.toml, Dockerfile creation, Dockerfile fixes (3), real pet context feature.

future_tasks.name: Cleanup — delete file_store.py, storage/, and data/ directory
future_tasks.description: file_store.py deprecated since Phase 1C (replaced by repositories.py). context.py deleted (W14, 2026-03-16). app/storage/ directory (file_store.py + __init__.py) and data/ directory (6 legacy files) deleted in Sprint 5.
future_tasks.notes: All deprecated files deleted. Zero imports confirmed by grep before deletion.

future_tasks.name: Wire x-user-code header — multi-user pet_id resolution
future_tasks.description: X-User-Code header required on all endpoints. chat.py extracts it for AALDA auth and user identity. pet_ids (integer list) in ChatRequest. All DEFAULT_PET_ID removed. Aggregator receives pet_id per request.

future_tasks.name: AALDA API integration — backend fetches pet data
future_tasks.description: PetFetcher calls AALDA API (GET /api/v1/pet-profile/{pet_id}) with X-User-Code auth. Maps AALDA fields to internal schema. In-memory TTL cache (5 min). Returns pet_profile + aalda_facts. New endpoint GET /api/v1/pets lists user's pets.

future_tasks.name: Remove DEFAULT_PET_ID and DEFAULT_USER_ID fallbacks
future_tasks.description: All DEFAULT_PET_ID and DEFAULT_USER_ID removed from constants.py, chat.py, debug.py, aggregator.py. pet_ids required in ChatRequest. X-User-Code header required (401 if missing). pet_id is integer everywhere.

future_tasks.name: Valkey hot storage — decision made (Valkey, not Redis)
future_tasks.description: Implement Valkey as hot storage for conversation threads and active profiles. Valkey is a Redis-compatible open-source fork — any Redis client library (redis-py, aioredis) works unchanged. Replaces in-memory Python dict (app.state.sessions, app.state.active_profile). Write-through to PostgreSQL already implemented in Phase 2. COORDINATE with ft-003 (multi-user) — both require restructuring app.state.
future_tasks.priority: high — required for horizontal scaling and production multi-user

future_tasks.name: Code review fixes — all review debt resolved (Sprint 2-3 + Sprint 4)
future_tasks.description: All review items from Sprint 2-3 and Sprint 4 resolved. Sprint 5 closed remaining 9 items: StateBag Protocol type hints, compaction summary label fix, deprecated file deletions (ft-002), frontend crypto.randomUUID + redirect whitelist, per-pet asyncio.Lock for thread creation race, partial unique index (one active thread per pet), FK constraint on thread_messages, background.py extraction from chat.py, parallel aggregator via asyncio.gather(). Only W18 (users table schema — other team) remains as external dependency.

future_tasks.name: Eliminate 4-second confidence bar delay
future_tasks.description: After a chat message, the Aggregator runs in the background (fire-and-forget). Frontend polls GET /confidence 4s later to pick up the updated score. Three options identified:
options: Option A: Recalculate confidence in _run_background() after Aggregator finishes, cache on app.state. GET /confidence returns cached value instantly. Simplest, no new infra.
Option B: SSE (Server-Sent Events) push. Backend pushes updated score to frontend when Aggregator finishes. Real-time, but adds SSE infrastructure.
Option C: Accept the 4-second delay. It works, users unlikely to notice. Revisit only if it becomes a UX issue.

future_tasks.name: API v1 — versioning, error contract, redirect restructure
future_tasks.description: Implement design-docs/api-v1-design.md. (1) Add /api/v1 prefix to all routers. (2) Remove pet_context from ChatRequest, add pet_id with DEFAULT_PET_ID fallback for dev. (3) Restructure RedirectPayload — remove deep_link URL, add display{label,style} + context{query,pet_id,pet_summary}. (4) Add custom error handler — all errors return {status:'error',error:{code,message}}. (5) Add status:'ok' to all success responses. (6) Update React frontend URLs + redirect handling. (7) Update tests + CLAUDE.md.

future_tasks.name: Multi-pet support — two pets per user simultaneously
future_tasks.description: ChatRequest accepts pet_ids: list[int] (1-2 pets). Dual-pet context pipeline: parallel AALDA fetch, per-pet active_profile from DB, per-pet gap sections. Prompt v0.3 with PET A / PET B sections. Frontend PetSelect with multi-select (max 2). Conversation agent updated for dual-pet.

future_tasks.name: Multiple LLM providers — OpenAI + Claude/Anthropic
future_tasks.description: Currently Azure OpenAI only. Add two new provider implementations: (1) OpenAI direct (not Azure), (2) Claude/Anthropic API. LLMProvider abstract class and factory pattern already exist — just need concrete implementations in app/llm/. Factory reads LLM_PROVIDER env var. Zero agent code changes needed.

future_tasks.name: Web frontend enhancements for staging
future_tasks.description: Getting a web version of frontend for internal/staging testing. Current React frontend (frontend/) exists as a test UI. May need enhancements: auth flow, multi-pet switching, better UX for internal testers.

future_tasks.name: HistoryBuilder — pet history narrative from fact_log
future_tasks.description: New LLM-based service (app/services/history_builder.py) that reads fact_log rows for a pet and builds a chronological narrative stored as _pet_history in active_profile. Similar pattern to ThreadSummarizer. Runs in _run_background() after Aggregator writes new facts. Focus on events and changes: health events, medication changes, behavioral shifts, vet visits, and time_scope='past' facts (which the Aggregator skips but are exactly what history needs). Prompt plumbing already wired — build_pet_context() extracts _pet_history, conversation.py template has {pet_history_section}.
future_tasks.notes: Prompt wiring done (2026-03-16). Only the builder itself + trigger in _run_background() remain.

future_tasks.name: SSE compaction notifications + message queuing
future_tasks.description: Use Server-Sent Events (SSE) to notify the frontend when compaction is running. While compaction is in progress for a thread, incoming messages for that thread are queued (not dropped, not blocked silently). Frontend shows a 'processing' state. Once compaction finishes, queued messages are processed and frontend is notified it can resume. This replaces the current pattern where the per-thread lock makes Request B wait silently during compaction.
future_tasks.notes: Per-thread asyncio.Lock (C2) is the current safety mechanism. SSE is the UX improvement on top — user sees 'compacting...' instead of a slow response. Design: SSE endpoint per thread, frontend subscribes on mount, server pushes compaction_start/compaction_done events.

summary.note: Phase 0-2 + API v1 + Sprint 2-5 complete. All review debt resolved (Sprint 2-3 + Sprint 4). ft-002 complete (all deprecated files deleted). 68/70 E2E tests pass (2 LLM non-determinism in Compressor). Next: ft-013 (HistoryBuilder), ft-005 (Valkey hot storage), Phase 3 (nightly batch jobs).
